Article Title: NFL Trade Rumors: Kansas City Chiefs linked to $12 Million Carolina Panthers WR in potential trade
Article Text:
The Kansas City Chiefs have been identified as a potential trade destination for Carolina Panthers wide receiver Xavier Legette, according to USA Today's Anthony Rizzuti. While Carolina has not indicated it wants to move the former first-round pick, Legette's inconsistent production has fueled speculation. Kansas City could benefit from adding receiver depth for Patrick Mahomes, though the Panthers are still expected to keep Legette unless a strong trade offer emerges before the deadline.





Category Details Player Xavier Legette Current Team Carolina Panthers Rumored Team Kansas City Chiefs Contract Status Rookie contract Salary Cap Hit Team-friendly rookie deal Trade Likelihood Moderate Latest Insider Update Listed by USA Today's Anthony Rizzuti as a potential Chiefs trade target Potential Return Mid-round NFL Draft pick (speculation)

Which teams are interested in Xavier Legette?

What insiders are saying about the trade rumors

Contract details and salary cap implications

How the trade could impact both teams

Trade speculation surrounding Carolina Panthers wide receiver Xavier Legette continues to gain attention as the 2026 NFL season approaches. While no formal talks have been reported, Legette has emerged as a possible trade candidate, with the Kansas City Chiefs recently identified as a potential landing spot. The 25-year-old remains on his rookie contract, making him an affordable option for teams seeking help at wide receiver.The Chiefs are among the teams being linked to Legette as questions continue to surround Carolina's receiving corps. Although there is no indication that Kansas City has made an offer, the fit has drawn attention because the Chiefs are still looking to add reliable playmakers around Patrick Mahomes.Legette entered the league as the final first-round pick in the 2024 NFL Draft and showed promise during his rookie campaign. He appeared in 16 games, recording 49 receptions for 497 yards and four touchdowns. However, his production dipped in 2025, finishing with 35 catches for 363 yards and three scores in 15 appearances.Even with that decline, his athletic profile and upside remain attractive. A change of scenery, especially in an offense led by Mahomes and Andy Reid, could provide the fresh start some evaluators believe he needs.Carolina, however, still has reasons to keep him. First-round pick Tetairoa McMillan is expected to lead the receiver room, while Legette and Jalen Coker remain important pieces behind him.The latest speculation gained traction after USA Today's Anthony Rizzuti listed four possible destinations if Carolina decides to move Legette. Kansas City stood out because of its need for another dependable receiver and its track record of helping young offensive players develop.At this stage, though, the rumors are driven by outside analysis rather than confirmed discussions. The Panthers have not indicated they are actively shopping Legette, and league insiders have not reported any ongoing negotiations.For now, Carolina appears prepared to give the former first-round selection another opportunity to prove he can become a consistent contributor.Legette remains on his rookie contract, giving any interested team the benefit of cost certainty. His deal carries a manageable salary cap hit, making him a low-risk option for clubs with limited financial flexibility.There are no reported no-trade clauses or contractual restrictions that would complicate a potential move. If Carolina eventually chooses to deal him, completing a trade would be relatively straightforward from a salary cap perspective.For Kansas City, adding Legette would strengthen receiver depth while giving Mahomes another young target with untapped potential. The Chiefs have consistently looked for affordable talent that can develop within Reid's offensive system.The Panthers would lose a former first-round pick but could gain future draft capital while creating additional opportunities for younger receivers such as Chris Brazzell II, Jimmy Horn Jr., and John Metchie III.With training camp approaching, Legette remains a player worth monitoring. Still, unless Carolina receives a compelling offer, keeping him on the roster through at least the trade deadline appears to be the more likely outcome.
Article Summary: Xavier Legette has emerged as a possible trade candidate, with the Kansas City Chiefs identified as a potential landing spot. The 25-year-old remains on his rookie contract, making him an affordable option for teams seeking help at wide receiver. No formal talks have been reported, and Carolina has not indicated it wants to move the former first-round pick.

Article Title: "I’m working hard": Brittany Mahomes says watching Patrick Mahomes inspired one emotional promise she made to their kids
Article Text:
Brittany Mahomes says becoming a mother has made her more ambitious and intentional about pursuing her goals. She hopes her children, Sterling, Bronze, and Golden, see both her and Patrick Mahomes working hard and following their dreams. Brittany believes leading by example will teach them that success comes through determination and perseverance. She also shared that her daughter Sterling has shown growing interest in her work, making those moments even more meaningful.

Brittany Mahomes says motherhood changed her perspective and strengthened her ambition

Brittany Mahomes hopes her children learn there are no limits to their dreams

Watching Patrick Mahomes excel on football's biggest stage is already a powerful lesson for the Mahomes children. But Brittany Mahomes believes that example should not come from just one parent. The Kansas City Current co-owner says becoming a mother has strengthened her drive to chase her own ambitions, hoping her children grow up seeing both parents work hard, pursue their passions, and never place limits on what they can achieve.Since welcoming her three children with Kansas City Chiefs quarterback Patrick Mahomes, Brittany says her outlook on life has evolved in ways she never expected. Motherhood has become more than a personal milestone. It has influenced how she approaches her career, her goals, and the example she wants to leave behind.She believes children learn by watching, which is why she wants Sterling, Bronze, and Golden to see their mother pursuing meaningful work with the same commitment they see from their father on the football field.ID@undefinedCaption not available.During an earlier interview, Brittany explained why that matters so much to her.“At the end of the day, my kids are watching me, and I want them to know that I’m working hard. I’m out here doing things as well, because they noticed their dad’s gone all the time,” she said, per Us Weekly. “And he’s working hard and doing stuff that he loves. So, I want them to see both of us going out and conquering the world.”That philosophy continues to shape how Brittany balances family life with her growing list of professional responsibilities. Rather than stepping away from her ambitions after becoming a mother, she says her children have inspired her to work even harder.Reflecting on how parenthood has changed her, Brittany shared, “Being a mom changed so many things in my life and changed how I looked at so many things. It really reshaped and redefined a lot of things in my life.”She explained that becoming a parent has made her more intentional with her time and more selective about opportunities that truly align with her values and long-term goals.Today, Brittany is expanding her own career by serving as Yahoo 's New Fans Correspondent during the World Cup, introducing new audiences to the sport she has loved for years. The role allows her to combine her passion for soccer with her desire to inspire the next generation, especially her own children.ID@undefinedCaption not available.She says her biggest motivation is showing them that determination and hard work can open countless doors.She said she’s “more motivated to keep going and keep showing my kids that there are no boundaries in front of you.” And as they see their mom going after her goals, she hopes they’ll learn: “You can achieve anything. You can be a part of anything that you want to. You need to dream big and understand that you have to work hard and do the hard things to do the fun things.”That lesson is already beginning to resonate at home. Brittany recently revealed that her oldest daughter, Sterling, showed genuine curiosity while watching her work on a soccer-related project, wanting to understand what her mother does outside of family life.The Mahomes family is always sharing cute moments on social media, showing how close Brittany, Patrick, and their kids are. While Patrick is still leading the Chiefs on the field, Brittany is building a legacy of her own, showing that success, ambition and parenthood can coexist. Her greatest achievement will not be just professional achievements. It will be raising kids who believe there is no dream too big to be pursued with commitment and perseverance.
Article Summary: Brittany Mahomes says becoming a mother has made her more ambitious and intentional about pursuing her goals. She hopes her children, Sterling, Bronze, and Golden, see both her and Patrick Mahomes working hard and following their dreams. The Kansas City Current co-owner is expanding her career by serving as Yahoo's New Fans Correspondent during the World Cup.

Article Title: “There’s so much drama and explanations": Blake Lively reportedly gets another chance as Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce's wedding rumors intensify
Article Text:
Rumors suggest Taylor Swift may be open to repairing her friendship with Blake Lively ahead of her reported July 3 wedding to Travis Kelce. Their relationship was reportedly strained after Swift's name surfaced in Blake Lively's legal dispute with Justin Baldoni over It Ends With Us. New reports claim Swift has extended an olive branch with a possible wedding invitation, though insiders say rebuilding trust will take time and nothing has been officially confirmed.

Taylor Swift and Blake Lively friendship reportedly on the road to reconciliation

Travis Kelce wedding rumors with Taylor Swift sparks new Blake Lively speculation

More rumors have emerged about Taylor Swift and Blake Lively ’s friendship, this time, reports claim that the pop superstar may be willing to give her longtime pal another shot. The speculation comes just days ahead of Swift's widely rumored wedding to Travis Kelce , with insiders claiming an invitation could be the first step towards repairing a relationship that has reportedly been under strain for months.For years, Taylor Swift and Blake Lively have been one of Hollywood’s most recognizable celebrity friendships. The couple were seen together at public appearances and private occasions. But the last few months have been filled with stories that their bond suffered after Swift’s name became tied to the legal fight between Blake Lively and filmmaker Justin Baldoni over It Ends With Us.Several reports say Swift was upset after private conversations became part of the larger legal story. She was never expecting to find herself in a legal battle over the movie and was upset to see her name dragged into the controversy, sources said.One of the most talked about moments came when text messages between the two friends leaked. The talks revealed a more intimate side to their relationship during a difficult period.In the exchange that was reported, Blake wrote that she had been overwhelmed by everything going on around her, writing that she felt like a “sad sack,” and a bad friend.Taylor’s reaction also gained attention when she allegedly said she missed her “funny, dark, normal-speaking friend” and that “there’s so much drama and so many explanations constantly, it’s just weird now,” which could have led to the emotional distance.Swift and Lively have not commented in detail on the reported messages, but the exchange has sparked widespread discussion on whether their friendship could survive such a public fallout.The latest buzz is that Swift is rumored to be planning a wedding to Kansas City Chiefs star Travis Kelce. Neither has confirmed the ceremony, but entertainment reports say preparations are afoot for a star-studded celebration that likely will include many of the couple's famous friends.Despite the recent tension insiders now claim Blake Lively could be invited. If true, the gesture would be more than an invitation to a wedding. It could be a sign that Swift is ready to rebuild trust after months of reported distance.Sources, however, warned that an invite doesn’t necessarily mean all is forgiven and forgotten over the issues that reportedly strained their friendship. It would be seen as an opportunity to start moving forward, with both sides slowly rebuilding trust.So far much of the discussion has been fueled by unnamed sources and online speculation, rather than official confirmation. The true status of their relationship is anyone’s guess until Swift, Kelce or Lively says something.Nevertheless, the prospect of a reunion has captured the imagination of fans. Whether Blake Lively goes to the rumored wedding or not, the story is a reminder that even the closest of celebrity friendships can be tested when private relationships become public.
Article Summary: Travis Kelce wedding rumors with Taylor Swift sparks new Blake Lively speculation. New reports claim Swift has extended an olive branch with a possible wedding invitation. Sources say rebuilding trust will take time and nothing has been officially confirmed. The true status of their relationship is anyone’s guess until Swift, Kelce or Lively says something.

Article Title: Kyle Pitts officially signs historic $54 million deal — but not everyone's convinced
Article Text:
Kyle Pitts officially signs historic $54 million deal (Image via: Getty Images)

Falcons make it official: Kyle Pitts signs NFL's biggest three-year TE deal amid doubts



Why Atlanta wanted him locked in



Not everyone's buying it, though



"How many times have we seen a guy have a career year in a contract year? How come you couldn't do that in the previous three years when the contract year wasn't there?"

"I don't love it because I don't think the player has lived up to what we thought he was going to be...

he was just going to be a matchup nightmare. He just hasn't been that just yet."

Kyle Pitts has put the contract drama to rest. The Atlanta Falcons tight end has officially signed his three-year, $54 million extension, locking him into the franchise through the 2028 season and ending months of speculation about his long-term future with the team.According to ESPN's Adam Schefter, the contract includes $36 million fully guaranteed, with an $18 million average annual value, making Pitts the third highest-paid tight end in the league, just behind San Francisco's George Kittle and Arizona's Trey McBride.It's officially the largest three-year contract for a tight end in NFL history. The deal replaces the one-year franchise tag Pitts had signed back in April, worth just over $15 million, and gives both sides certainty heading into the 2026 season after the two parties had until July 15 to strike a long-term agreement.Pitts was the No. 4 overall pick in the 2021 draft, a controversial selection at the time given how rarely tight ends go that high. He repaid that faith almost immediately, racking up 1,026 receiving yards as a rookie, the first 1,000-yard season by a tight end since 1961, and earning a Pro Bowl nod. The three seasons that followed were rockier, with injuries and inconsistency keeping him under 700 yards each year. But 2025 marked a real turnaround: 88 catches, 928 yards and five touchdowns, good enough for an All-Pro Second Team nod and clearly enough to convince the Falcons he's worth building around again.ESPN's Booger McFarland didn't hold back when asked about the deal, pointing to Pitts' habit of producing only when his pay cheque is on the line:His verdict:There's also a quarterback problem hanging over all of it. The Falcons head into training camp with Tua Tagovailoa and Michael Penix Jr. fighting for the starting job, with Penix still working back from his third career ACL tear. Pitts' production has swung wildly depending on who's throwing him the ball in the past, so his next big season is far from guaranteed. And the spending is starting to add up: alongside Drake London's four-year, $141 million deal from earlier this month, and a Bijan Robinson extension reportedly next on the list, Atlanta could end up committing over $74 million in guarantees to just three players, while their entire quarterback room currently costs the team a mere $4.42 million.
Article Summary: Atlanta Falcons tight end Kyle Pitts has signed a three-year, $54 million contract extension with the team. The deal includes $36 million fully guaranteed, with an $18 million average annual value. It's the largest contract for a tight end in NFL history. Pitts was the No. 4 overall pick in the 2021 draft.

Article Text:
Jonathan Kuminga. Image via: Joe Boatman/ NBAE via Getty Images

Jonathan Kuminga becomes free agent after Hawks reject $24.3 million option

Jonathan Kuminga's current situation

The Jonathan Kuminga saga took another unexpected turn Monday after the Atlanta Hawks declined the forward's $24.3 million team option for the 2026-27 season. It has prompted the 23-year-old to become an unrestricted free agent. The decision, first reported by ESPN and later confirmed by NBA insider Chris Haynes, comes less than five months after Atlanta acquired Kuminga from the Golden State Warriors.The move immediately reshapes both Kuminga's future and Atlanta's offseason strategy. While the Hawks can still negotiate a new multi-year deal with the athletic forward, declining the option gives both sides flexibility as free agency begins and opens the door for rival teams to pursue one of the league's youngest available wings.Atlanta's decision may appear abrupt, but it aligns with the franchise's recent emphasis on maintaining salary flexibility while continuing to reshape its roster.According to Chris Haynes, "Atlanta Hawks are declining the $24.3M team option on Johnathan Kuminga for the 2026-27 season and he becomes an unrestricted free agent, league sources tell me."The Hawks acquired Kuminga and veteran sharpshooter Buddy Hield from Golden State in February in exchange for Kristaps Porzingis.At the time, the trade offered Kuminga a fresh opportunity after his role with the Warriors steadily diminished.His first impression in Atlanta was encouraging. Kuminga scored 27 points in his Hawks debut and showed flashes of the explosive athleticism that made him the seventh overall pick in the 2021 NBA Draft. Over 16 regular-season games, he averaged 12.3 points, 5.3 rebounds and 2.1 assists while shooting 47.6 percent from the field.Atlanta also saw glimpses of his potential during its first-round playoff series against the eventual champion New York Knicks. Kuminga averaged 13.7 points across six postseason games, although his production fluctuated and outside shooting remained inconsistent, including a 5-for-24 performance from beyond the arc during the series.Rather than commit $24.3 million for one season, the Hawks appear to have chosen financial flexibility over a guaranteed salary that exceeded many league evaluations of Kuminga's current market value.Declining the option does not necessarily signal the end of Kuminga's time in Atlanta.The Hawks remain eligible to negotiate a new long-term contract with the versatile forward, potentially at a lower annual salary. Such an arrangement could benefit both parties by giving Atlanta greater cap flexibility while offering Kuminga long-term security.The organization has already been active entering free agency. Atlanta acquired Aaron Wiggins from the Oklahoma City Thunder and added Devin Carter in a trade with the Sacramento Kings while also exercising Buddy Hield's contract option. Those moves have created a crowded roster and increased pressure to manage payroll efficiently.Kuminga now reaches unrestricted free agency for the first time in his career after an eventful 12 months.He signed a two-year, $48.5 million contract with Golden State before requesting a trade in January as his playing time declined under head coach Steve Kerr. Reports throughout his final months with the Warriors suggested frustration over his inconsistent role, eventually leading to the February trade.Monday's decision also reignited debate around Kuminga's long-term value. Some league observers questioned whether he had done enough to justify a $24.3 million salary after his brief Atlanta stint, while others believe his age, athleticism and scoring upside remain attractive enough to command significant interest elsewhere.Although his stay with the Hawks lasted only a few months, Kuminga produced respectable numbers while adjusting to a new system.Atlanta's choice not to exercise the option reflects financial strategy more than a definitive judgment on his talent. As free agency opens, Kuminga becomes one of the more intriguing young wings available, and his next contract will reveal how the rest of the league values his combination of youth, athleticism and untapped potential.
Article Summary: Atlanta Hawks decline Jonathan Kuminga's $24.3 million team option for 2026-27 season. The move comes less than five months after the Hawks acquired the forward from the Golden State Warriors. The Hawks can still negotiate a new multi-year deal with the 23-year-old forward.

Article Title: Ja Morant trade: What justifies Memphis Grizzlies and Portland Trail Blazers deal?
Article Text:
Ja Morant. Image via: Joe Murphy/NBAE via Getty Images

Memphis Grizzlies traded Ja Morant to Portland Trail Blazers



Portland Trail Blazers receive:

Ja Morant

What the trade means for Memphis Grizzlies?



What the trade means for Portland Trail Blazers?



The Ja Morant trade became one of the defining moves of the NBA offseason. The Memphis Grizzlies reportedly agreed to send the two-time All-Star guard to the Portland Trail Blazers in exchange for veteran forward Jerami Grant and young wing Kris Murray. ESPN's Shams Charania first reported the deal on June 29, signaling the official end of Morant's seven-year run in Memphis.The move reshapes two franchises heading in opposite directions. Memphis commits fully to a youth-driven rebuild around Cameron Boozer, Zach Edey and Cedric Coward, while Portland bets that Morant can rediscover the form that once made him one of the NBA's brightest young superstars.After months of speculation, the Grizzlies finally acted on a decision that had become increasingly expected across the league. Memphis had already traded Desmond Bane to the Orlando Magic in 2025 and Jaren Jackson Jr. to the Utah Jazz earlier this year, making it clear that a new era was underway.The reported deal is straightforward.Notably, Memphis did not receive any draft picks despite moving one of the league's most recognizable guards. On paper, that return appears modest for a player who was once viewed as a franchise cornerstone and multiple first-round picks' worth of value.The circumstances, however, had changed dramatically. Morant's injuries, off-court issues and declining leverage around the league reportedly limited Memphis' options. Reports suggested some teams wanted draft compensation attached simply to absorb his contract. Instead, the Grizzlies managed to move him without surrendering future assets.Grant brings immediate scoring after averaging 18.6 points last season, while Murray, the former No. 23 overall pick, remains a developmental wing entering the final year of his rookie contract. Neither is widely viewed as a centerpiece of Memphis' long-term plans, but both offer roster flexibility as the organization reshapes its identity.The financial side of the trade helps explain why both organizations were willing to move forward.Morant remains under the five-year, $197.2 million extension he signed in 2022. He is scheduled to earn approximately $42.16 million during the 2026-27 season before making $44.88 million in the final guaranteed year of his contract.Grant still has two seasons remaining on his five-year, $160 million contract, including a player option worth $36.4 million for 2027-28. Murray will earn roughly $5.3 million in the final season of his rookie deal before becoming a restricted free agent next summer.From Memphis' perspective, swapping Morant's contract for Grant and Murray also creates greater long-term flexibility while continuing its roster reset.This trade officially closes one of the most eventful chapters in franchise history.Morant elevated Memphis into Western Conference contention, earning two All-Star selections and establishing himself among the league's elite young point guards. Yet injuries repeatedly interrupted that rise.He appeared in only 79 games over the past three seasons because of suspensions and multiple injuries, including a shoulder labral tear, hip issues, hamstring problems and most recently a UCL sprain that limited him to just 20 games during the 2025-26 campaign.Those availability concerns, combined with Memphis' broader rebuilding strategy, ultimately made a separation increasingly logical.The Grizzlies now appear committed to constructing their future around Cameron Boozer, Zach Edey and Cedric Coward while evaluating which veterans remain part of the next competitive core.For Portland, the trade represents a calculated gamble rather than a complete roster overhaul.The Trail Blazers returned to the playoffs last season behind first-time All-Star Deni Avdija and now add one of basketball's most explosive guards. According to NBA insider Chris Haynes, Portland intends to pair Morant with Damian Lillard in the starting backcourt."The Portland Trail Blazers envision a starting backcourt of Damian Lillard and Ja Morant alongside Deni Avdija on the wing," he reported . "A very unconventional move that the franchise believes has upside. Ja Morant gets his fresh start. Jerami Grant and Kris Murray off to Memphis."Questions remain about fit. Portland already features Lillard, Jrue Holiday, Scoot Henderson and Shaedon Sharpe, creating one of the league's deepest guard rotations. The acquisition could also influence the organization's reported interest in pursuing Boston Celtics star Jaylen Brown, with Yahoo Sports' Kevin O'Connor suggesting Morant's arrival may alter those plans.Ultimately, Portland is betting that a healthier Morant can regain the explosiveness that made him an All-NBA talent. If he does, the Trail Blazers may have acquired the highest-upside player moved during this offseason. If injuries continue to limit him, additional roster changes could become inevitable before the franchise fully realizes its long-term vision.
Article Summary: Memphis Grizzlies traded Ja Morant to Portland Trail Blazers on June 29. The two-time All-Star guard was traded in exchange for Jerami Grant and Kris Murray. The move reshapes two franchises heading in opposite directions. Memphis commits fully to a youth-driven rebuild around Cameron Boozer, Zach Edey and Cedric Coward.

Article Text:

Lakers emerge as latest team to monitor Duren's situation



Detroit Pistons center Jalen Duren. (AP Photo/Ryan Sun)

Contract negotiations remain the central obstacle



Detroit still holds leverage, but time is becoming a factor



Detroit Pistons center Jalen Duren (0) talks with head coach J.B. Bickerstaff during the first half in Game 7 of a second-round NBA playoffs basketball series Sunday, May 17, 2026, in Detroit. (AP Photo/Duane Burleson)

A defining decision awaits the Pistons



The Detroit Pistons entered the offseason expecting contract talks with Jalen Duren to be a formality. Instead, one of the franchise's foundational pieces has become one of the NBA's most intriguing restricted free agents, with multiple contenders now exploring ways to pry him away from Detroit.Just days after reports connected Duren to the Sacramento Kings, another Western Conference contender has entered the picture. According to The Athletic's Sam Amick, the Los Angeles Lakers have scheduled a meeting with the 22-year-old center following the opening of free agency, creating yet another potential sign-and-trade pathway while negotiations between Duren and the Pistons remain unresolved.Here’s what complicates things a little: Detroit still possesses considerable leverage because Duren is a restricted free agent, but every additional suitor increases the pressure on the Pistons' front office to determine exactly how much it is willing to invest in one of the league's fastest-rising young centers.The Lakers' reported interest comes after Sacramento had already positioned itself as the most aggressive challenger for Duren's services.Earlier reports indicated the Kings planned to meet with Duren at the outset of free agency while discussing a potential sign-and-trade package that could send three-time All-Star Domantas Sabonis to Detroit. Although no formal offer has been finalized, the framework illustrates just how highly rival executives value Duren after his breakout campaign.Los Angeles, meanwhile, has not been linked to a specific trade proposal. Even so, the franchise's interest is understandable.The Lakers have spent recent seasons searching for a long-term answer in the middle. Duren's combination of size, athleticism, rim protection and efficient finishing would immediately fill that void while also providing a young cornerstone capable of growing alongside the franchise's next era.For Detroit, however, every new conversation involving Duren complicates an offseason that was initially centered on building around him and franchise guard Cade Cunningham.The increased interest follows reports that negotiations between Duren and the Pistons have stalled.NBA insider Chris Haynes reported that both sides remain significantly apart financially, with Duren reportedly underwhelmed by Detroit's initial contract proposal. That impasse has fueled discussions about alternative destinations rather than a straightforward extension.Detroit's front office has consistently maintained that retaining Duren remains its priority, but the financial equation has changed dramatically after his career-best season.The former first-round pick averaged 19.5 points and 10.5 rebounds across 70 games while shooting an impressive 65 percent from the field. His emergence earned him his first All-Star selection, All-NBA Third Team honors and votes for Defensive Player of the Year.Those accolades also carry major contractual implications.Because Duren qualified for All-NBA honors, he became eligible for a Rose Rule extension that could reach approximately 30 percent of the salary cap. Reports have also suggested his camp is seeking a deal approaching $40 million annually, a figure that reflects both his production and the rapidly escalating market for elite young centers.Unlike unrestricted free agents, Duren cannot simply choose his next destination.As a restricted free agent, the Pistons retain matching rights on outside offers, giving them substantial control over the process. That leverage often leads teams to retain young stars, but sign-and-trade discussions introduce another layer of complexity.If Detroit ultimately determines that Duren's asking price exceeds its long-term payroll plans, negotiating a trade could allow the franchise to recover significant assets instead of risking a prolonged contract dispute.Sacramento's reported interest in constructing a deal around Sabonis demonstrates the caliber of return Detroit could potentially command. The Lakers have yet to reveal what assets they would be willing to include, but their involvement further strengthens Duren's negotiating position.Few players have boosted their market value more over the past year than Duren.His regular-season breakout transformed him from a promising young center into one of the league's premier interior talents, even though his production dipped during Detroit's postseason run. Around the NBA, executives continue to view him as a player entering his prime rather than one whose value diminished in the playoffs.For the Pistons, the coming days could shape the direction of the franchise for years.They can meet Duren's contract demands and continue building around their young core, or they can entertain increasingly serious sign-and-trade discussions with teams eager to capitalize on the current uncertainty.
Article Summary: Detroit Pistons center Jalen Duren has become one of the NBA's most intriguing restricted free agents. The 22-year-old averaged 19.5 points and 10.5 rebounds across 70 games while shooting an impressive 65 percent from the field. His emergence earned him his first All-Star selection, All-NBA Third Team honors and votes for Defensive Player of the Year.

Article Title: At 13, Jimmy Butler was kicked out by his mom; that day marked the beginning of one of the NBA's greatest underdog journeys
Article Text:
Jimmy Butler's remarkable journey from homelessness to NBA superstardom (Getty image)

Jimmy Butler's underdog journey began the day he lost his home







Basketball became Jimmy Butler's way forward



Jimmy Butler's NBA career: From the 30th pick to one of basketball's biggest stars



Jimmy Butler's career only added to his remarkable legacy

Jimmy Butler's personal life away from basketball

Jimmy Butler's career did not begin with packed NBA arenas or multimillion-dollar contracts. It began with heartbreak. At just 13 years old, Butler found himself without a home after his mother told him she no longer wanted him around. For years, he moved from one friend's couch to another, carrying little more than hope and a dream that seemed impossible at the time. Few could have thought that the same teenager would one day become one of basketball's biggest stars.Now, Jimmy Butler is recognized as one of the NBA's fiercest competitors. Behind every playoff performance, All-Star selection and lucrative contract is a journey built on rejection, sacrifice and resilience. Long before fans knew him as "Jimmy Buckets," Butler was simply a teenager trying to survive while refusing to give up on basketball.Jimmy Butler never had the childhood most future NBA stars enjoy. Born in Houston, Texas, he grew up without his father, who left when Butler was still an infant. The biggest turning point came at the age of 13 when, according to Butler, his mother looked at him and said, "I don't like the look of you.You gotta go."With nowhere to live, Butler spent the next several years moving from one friend's house to another. Some nights he had a place to sleep. Other nights he had to figure things out on his own. Yet even during those uncertain years, basketball remained the one constant in his life. It gave him a purpose and something worth chasing.Everything began to change when Butler became close friends with Jordan Leslie, who would later play in the NFL. Leslie's mother, Michelle Lambert, welcomed Butler into her home despite already raising several children. She did not treat him like a guest. She treated him like family.Years later, Butler admitted he struggled to believe someone would show him that kind of kindness after everything he had experienced growing up. Living with the Lambert family gave him something he had been missing for years: stability. It helped him focus on school, mature as a person and devote himself to basketball.Despite the hardships, Butler has never wanted sympathy. Speaking to ESPN years later, he reflected on his childhood by saying, "There's nothing to feel sorry about. I love what happened to me. It made me who I am. I'm grateful for the challenges I've faced." That mindset would define the rest of his life. Every setback became another reason to work harder.Even after finding stability, Butler remained overlooked by major college programs. Playing for Tomball High School, he averaged nearly 20 points during his senior year but attracted little recruiting attention and was rated only a two-star prospect.Without scholarship offers from powerhouse schools, he enrolled at Tyler Junior College in Texas. Many players would have viewed junior college as a setback. Butler viewed it as another opportunity to prove people wrong.His performances at Tyler eventually earned him a scholarship to Marquette University. There, he steadily transformed from an athletic prospect into a complete player. By his senior season, he was averaging 15.7 points per game and earning All-Big East Honorable Mention recognition.Still, NBA scouts remained unconvinced. Butler was never viewed as a future superstar. He entered the 2011 NBA Draft with far less hype than many players selected ahead of him.Jimmy Butler's professional career began quietly when the Chicago Bulls selected him with the 30th overall pick in the 2011 NBA Draft. His rookie season offered few opportunities. Playing behind an experienced roster, Butler spent much of the year on the bench. Rather than becoming frustrated, he used every practice to improve.That patience soon paid off. Over the next three seasons, Butler developed into one of the league's premier two-way players. His relentless defense, improving offensive game and unmatched work ethic quickly made him indispensable for Chicago.The breakthrough came during the 2014-15 season when Butler averaged more than 20 points per game and won the NBA Most Improved Player Award, becoming the first Bulls player ever to receive the honor. Chicago rewarded him with a five-year, $95 million contract extension.After six seasons with the Bulls, Butler was traded to the Minnesota Timberwolves in 2017, where he helped end the franchise's long playoff drought. One season later, he moved to the Philadelphia 76ers before signing with the Miami Heat in 2019. Miami became the perfect fit.Known for demanding accountability from teammates and setting exceptionally high standards, Butler quickly established himself as the leader of Erik Spoelstra's squad. Under his leadership, the Heat reached multiple NBA Finals appearances despite often entering the postseason as underdogs.His playoff performances earned him even greater respect around the league, with fans embracing the nickname "Jimmy Buckets" for his ability to elevate his game when the stakes were highest.Many folks thought that Butler's breakout season in Chicago would be the peak of his career. Instead, it became only the beginning.After winning the NBA's Most Improved Player award in 2015, Butler established himself among the league's elite two-way players. His career later took him to Minnesota and Philadelphia before he found his ideal home in Miami.In Miami, Butler developed his fame as one of basketball's fiercest competitors. He led the Heat to multiple NBA Finals appearances, earned six NBA All-Star selections, five All-NBA Team honors and five NBA All-Defensive Team selections while becoming the face of the franchise. His relentless playoff performances eventually turned "Jimmy Buckets" into one of the NBA's most recognizable nicknames.Jimmy Butler's financial journey mirrors his rise in basketball. From a player who struggled to attract scholarship offers, he has become one of the NBA's highest-paid stars. According to HoopsHype, Butler has earned approximately $366.8 million in NBA salary through the 2025-26 season, with another $56.83 million scheduled for 2026-27. Celebrity Net Worth estimates his personal net worth at around $80 million, with additional wealth coming from endorsements, investments and business ventures.Source: HoopsHype(career earnings and net worth estimates).Away from the court, Butler prefers to keep much of his personal life private. He shares children with his former partner, Kaitlin Nowak, and has often spoken about how fatherhood has changed his perspective.During the NBA's pandemic bubble in 2020, Butler unexpectedly launched Big Face Coffee, selling cups of coffee to fellow players. What began as a lighthearted idea has since grown into a legitimate business venture that reflects another side of his personality. Butler is also known for his love of fashion, music and fine wine. Despite enjoying the rewards of success, he has never forgotten where he came from.Perhaps most surprisingly, Butler has said he holds no resentment toward either of his parents. Over the years, he has maintained contact with them and has chosen forgiveness over bitterness.Jimmy Butler's story has never been just about basketball. It is about a kid who refused to let rejection define his future. A boy who lost his home at 13 eventually built a career that placed him among the NBA's elite, earning hundreds of millions of dollars through determination alone. Championships, All-Star appearances and lucrative contracts are all part of Butler's legacy. But what continues to inspire fans is the road he traveled to reach them. Long before he became one of basketball's biggest stars, Jimmy Butler learned how to survive when life offered him almost nothing. That resilience remains the defining chapter of one of the NBA's greatest success stories.
Article Summary: Jimmy Butler's NBA career did not begin with packed NBA arenas or multimillion- dollar contracts. At just 13 years old, Butler found himself without a home after his mother told him she no longer wanted him around. Butler remained overlooked by major college programs until he enrolled at Tyler Junior College.

Article Title: Malik Beasley indictment explained, federal prosecutors accuse former NBA star and Ed Davis in alleged betting operation
Article Text:
Malik Beasley (Image Via Getty)

Malik Beasley and Ed Davis named in federal indictment over alleged betting scheme

Malik Beasley’s financial problems came under focus before the indictment

Former NBA players Malik Beasley and Ed Davis are among six people indicted in a federal case tied to an alleged illegal sports betting operation, according to ESPN, which cited an indictment unsealed on Monday. Prosecutors claim Beasley agreed to change parts of his on-court performance during the 2023-24 NBA season while with the Milwaukee Bucks so others could place winning bets on his player statistics. The case also names NBA agent Paolo Zamorano and three other defendants.According to the indictment, Davis, Rob Gorodetsky, Ernesto Plascencia and William Brown were taken into custody on Monday. Beasley and Paolo Zamorano, who previously worked as Davis’ agent, were not in custody as of Monday morning, according to the U.S. Attorney’s Office for the Eastern District of New York.Federal prosecutors allege that Beasley, who earned nearly $60 million during his nine-year NBA career, lost millions through gambling. They claim he agreed in advance to intentionally underperform or overperform in at least three Milwaukee Bucks games during the 2023-24 season so members of the alleged betting operation could place wagers on his player prop bets.The indictment states: ”[T]he defendants and their co-conspirators placed numerous fraudulent wagers totaling tens of thousands of dollars conditioned on defendant Malik Beasley’s ‘under rebounds’ prop bets.”One example in the indictment points to a Bucks game in which Beasley finished with three rebounds, below the 3.5 betting line that was available at several sportsbooks. Prosecutors say the wagers were linked to that performance.The new indictment is separate from earlier gambling-related cases involving former NBA players Jontay Porter, Terry Rozier and Damon Jones, according to ESPN.Beasley has also dealt with financial issues away from basketball. In March, a judge ordered him to pay $1 million to his former marketing agency after a dispute over an unpaid $650,000 advance. Court records also show he was evicted from his Detroit apartment last summer after his landlord sued over more than $21,000 in unpaid rent.The federal investigation first became public while Beasley was negotiating a reported three-year, $42 million contract with the Detroit Pistons last year. The deal was never completed after news of the investigation surfaced. ESPN first reported the indictment, while the U.S. Attorney’s Office for the Eastern District of New York announced the charges on Monday.
Article Summary: Malik Beasley and Ed Davis are among six people indicted in a federal case tied to an alleged illegal sports betting operation. Prosecutors claim Beasley agreed to change parts of his on-court performance during the 2023-24 NBA season so others could place winning bets on his player statistics. The case also names NBA agent Paolo Zamorano and three other defendants.

What is Jalen Brunson doing this offseason?



Jalen Brunson is unstoppable. The New York Knicks star has just added another major accolade to a career-defining summer. He secured the Sportsman of the Year award at the 2026 BET Awards. The Knicks captain earned the honor weeks after leading the franchise to its first NBA championship in 53 years and claiming NBA Finals MVP, finishing ahead of a field that included LeBron James , Stephen Curry and Anthony Edwards.Brunson's championship run transformed the Knicks into title winners for the first time since 1973, and his growing influence continues to extend well beyond the court during an eventful offseason.Jalen Brunson's victory at the BET Awards came against one of the strongest Sportsman of the Year fields in recent memory.The nominees included Aaron Judge, Anthony Edwards, Caleb Williams, Jalen Hurts, LeBron James, Shedeur Sanders, and Stephen Curry. Brunson ultimately emerged as the winner after completing one of the most memorable individual seasons in Knicks history.His case was built on much more than regular-season excellence.Brunson led New York to the NBA championship by defeating the San Antonio Spurs in five games, producing perhaps the defining performance of his career in the title clincher. He erupted for 45 points in Game 5, including 13 consecutive points in the fourth quarter, helping erase another double-digit deficit as the Knicks completed a 94-90 comeback victory.The championship ended a 53-year title drought for one of the NBA's flagship franchises.His championship season already featured an NBA Finals MVP trophy, a title parade through Manhattan and countless celebrations with Knicks fans who had waited more than five decades to witness another championship.Brunson stood at the center of every major moment. During New York's championship parade, thousands lined the streets to celebrate the point guard who changed the direction of the franchise after arriving four years earlier.Brunson continued to perform at an elite level throughout the 2025-26 regular season.Even after delivering New York's long-awaited championship, Brunson has remained active away from basketball.The Knicks star recently surprised actress Mariska Hargitay by attending her Broadway performance in Every Brilliant Thing at New York City's Hudson Theatre. Brunson watched from the audience before meeting Hargitay backstage alongside his wife, Ali Marks, while Hargitay was joined by her husband, Peter Hermann. According to People, the couples later shared dinner following the performance.Brunson has previously said that appearing on an episode of Law & Order: SVU would be a dream offseason opportunity. After a championship, a Finals MVP, a citywide parade and now a BET Sportsman of the Year award, that cameo may become one of the few remaining boxes left for New York's newest sports icon to check.
Article Summary: Jalen Brunson has just added another major accolade to a career-defining summer. He secured the Sportsman of the Year award at the 2026 BET Awards. The Knicks captain earned the honor weeks after leading the franchise to its first NBA championship in 53 years.

Article Title: 'The right heroes, the right infrastructure': NBA's roadmap for Indian basketball
Article Text:
At the NBA Rising Stars Invitational 2026, India's defeats revealed more than the gap to Asia's best. For NBA India Head Sunny Malik, the bigger story is the pathway now taking shape - from Jr. NBA to RSI and beyond - as the league looks to build an ecosystem that consistently produces elite basketball talent.

TimesofIndia.com in SINGAPORE:

Image Credit: NBA Rising Star Invitational

Where The Difference Really Lies



Sunny Malik (Image Credit: NBA Rising Star Invitational )

Building The Missing Pathway



Beyond that lies another possibility.

When Exposure Becomes Opportunity



Sheila Rasu (Image Credit: NBA Rising Star Invitational)

NBA Rising Star Invitational

Strengthening The Basketball Ecosystem



NBA Rising Star Invitational

More Than One Hero



NBA's roadmap for Indian basketball (Image: NBA Rising Star Invitational)

For nearly two quarters against Indonesia’s Jubliee School in their opening game of NBA ’s Rising Star Invitational 2026 in Singapore, the Velammal International School looked capable of competing. India's lone representatives moved the ball confidently and defended with intent, for spells.Then the pressure arrived. The pace quickened, the legs tired, and the margin steadily widened. By the final buzzer, the scoreboard read 95-61.In their second game against South Korea's Kyungbock High School - one of Asia's strongest school programmes - and the eventual winners, the relentless full-court press squeezed every possession. Passing lanes disappeared. Turnovers increased. Every defensive rebound quickly became another attack.By the time the final buzzer sounded, Velammal had suffered a 131-46 defeat.Head coach Shamsher Basha didn't dwell on the scoreline. Instead, he identified the problem straight up. "Yes, endurance was an issue," Basha told Timesofindia.com.His point guard, Fyodor Prem Athithan, noticed something even more fundamental."They were pressing full court," he said. "Back in India, there was no full-court press, only zone defence. Here, there is full-court pressure, so next time we should be able to handle it better and make the right moves."Former NBA Academy India recruit Kushal Singh reached a similar conclusion."We know that the other countries are better in basketball, so we get better competition to play against," he said.Over six days inside Singapore's OCBC Arena, those were similar patterns.Watching Japanese, South Korean, Chinese and Australian schools compete, the differences weren't confined to height or athleticism. The ball rarely stayed still whether teams were protecting a narrow lead or comfortably ahead.The gap wasn't simply one of talent. It was one of exposure.And for NBA India Head Sunny Malik, that is exactly why tournaments like the NBA Rising Stars Invitational have become so important."RSI is an amazing platform for high-school players," Malik told TimesofIndia.com."It gives them the opportunity to compete against the best teams in Asia. It provides tremendous exposure and allows young players to understand where they stand compared to the best in the region."For the Indian team that has participated, I think it is a great learning platform and an important step in helping them improve every year and eventually become regular competitors on the circuit."When asked specifically about India's struggles against South Korea's full-court press, Malik's answer echoed exactly what Basha and Fyodor had already described."I think more practice games in the full-court format would go a long way. Looking at the Indian team, they competed well in the first and second quarters. However, once the game entered the third quarter, they started losing steam, and that's when they also lost momentum."Longer-format practice, more competitive games, greater exposure against stronger teams, and increased competition within India can all go a long way in helping them compete consistently at this level."For much of the past decade, the NBA's grassroots presence in India has largely revolved around one programme - Jr. NBA. It introduced thousands of children to the game, took coaches into schools across the country and became the league's biggest grassroots initiative in India. But, what happened after that?For many young players, the pathway beyond school basketball was never clearly defined. That, Malik believes, is finally beginning to change."We have been running our Junior NBA programme for the last 13 years, and I believe it is a great platform for kids to get exposure to the right style of basketball and develop the right mindset to move up the ladder," he said."But from this year onwards, we are trying to increase the age group from Under-14 to Under-16. That gives us a much wider player pool, creates greater competition, and allows more players who have progressed through the Under-14 programme to continue competing in Junior NBA Under-16."That additional exposure will help prepare them for the level of basketball they need to play if they want to compete internationally."The change may appear incremental, but in reality, it is part of a much bigger shift in how the NBA views player development in India.With the NBA Rising Stars Invitational now completing its second edition, the league is beginning to stitch together a pathway that previously did not exist.A player can now enter the system through Junior NBA, continue competing at the Under-16 level, graduate into the proposed NBA Rising Stars Invitational Qualifier and, if successful, earn the opportunity to compete against the best school teams in Asia at the Rising Stars Invitational."The RSI Qualifier... is definitely on the cards," Malik revealed."We'd love to build around an RSI Qualifier because having the best teams qualify through a competition brings much more energy and credibility to the final teams than having them come through nominations or selections by the Basketball Federation of India."The NBA is trying to create continuity, and the league's ambition is no longer simply to introduce children to basketball. It is trying to ensure talented youngsters remain within a competitive system as they grow older.That pathway, according to Sheila Rasu, NBA's Head of Southeast Asia and Asia Marketing, is already beginning to produce tangible results across the region."I don't think that's something for the future," Rasu said when asked whether the Rising Stars Invitational could become a stepping stone to elite basketball."I think it's already happening."She pointed to last year's tournament, where scouts identified five girls who were subsequently invited to to Baketball Without Borders, the NBA and FIBA’s global basketball development and community outreach program."So it's already happening. We have scouts attending every year, and we're already using this tournament as a platform to identify young and promising talent."Rasu offered another example that perhaps best illustrates what the NBA hopes this competition can eventually become."Last year, Yongsan High School from South Korea participated, and the MVP of that team went on immediately afterwards to sign a professional contract in the Korean Basketball League. He has now also made the national team."Daniel Edi was first seen at our event, and then went on not only to sign a professional contract but also to represent his country. There are going to be more stories like that, and we want to create more stories like that."Edi was drafted by the Seoul SK Knights in the Korean Basketball League (KBL), making history as the first-ever player selected straight from high school through the KBL's Local Draft Pick system.He went on to debut for the Korea Men’s National Team during Window 2 of the FIBA Basketball World Cup 2027 Asian Qualifiers earlier this year.And perhaps with results like the above, will the conversations around college basketball, professional leagues and eventually the NBA become realistic and that long-term thinking is precisely why Malik insists the conversation should not revolve around one exceptional player."I completely agree," he said when asked about the importance of developing coaches alongside players."Ecosystem development is just as important as player development."You need the federation, the associations and all the relevant stakeholders to come together and invest in coach development because that's where the actual skill development begins. That's where the real foundation is built."That foundation has to be strong if we want to consistently produce great players from India."It is a philosophy shared across the NBA's operations in Asia. "The ecosystem is not just about the players," Rasu explained."Of course, the most important thing is helping players improve their skills, but you also need to improve the overall standard of the game. That means developing coaches, referees and exposing more young people to basketball."The referee and coaching clinics help raise the overall standard of the basketball community, so that as more tournaments emerge, there is enough expertise within the ecosystem to support them."The difference between India's representatives and the continent's leading school programmes was rarely about effort. Velammal competed, fought for loose balls and continued playing with energy even when the scoreline drifted away.Malik believes stronger domestic competition will be just as important."We also need stronger competition at the national level. With some of the changes that are being planned nationally, including new leagues that are being discussed, there may be more opportunities for Indians to connect with basketball and become more engaged with the sport."By the final day in Singapore, the tournament had produced champions, individual awards and memorable performances.For India's lone representatives, however, the most valuable takeaway lay elsewhere. Coach Basha spoke about endurance. Fyodor spoke about facing a full-court press he rarely encounters back home. Kaushal spoke about finally understanding where his team stood against the best in Asia.Malik connected those experiences into something much larger."I don't think there is just one thing that can bring a large audience to basketball," he said. "It has to be a combination of factors."Having the right player represent India on the global stage would certainly be one important step. We celebrate heroes in this country, and we've seen that with cricket."So, having the right heroes, combined with the right infrastructure and ecosystem, will help us build generations of players. We need a system that consistently produces talent, not just one player who reaches the global stage, but many players coming through regularly."
Article Summary: NBA's Rising Star Invitational 2026 was held in Singapore. India's high-school team was beaten 131-46 by South Korea's Kyungbock High School. Coach Shamsher Basha said full-court pressure was an issue for India's team. NBA India Head Sunny Malik believes the pathway is now taking shape to build an ecosystem that produces elite talent.
